data.title: Anthropic’s safety warnings may have just backfired — the government has pulled the plug on its most powerful AI
data.summary: Anthropic isn't hiding its frustration. "We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people," the company wrote in a blog post.

data.title: SpaceX IPO: Live updates on everything you need to know
data.summary: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

data.title: Meta’s months-old AI unit is a soul-crushing gulag, say the engineers stuck inside it
data.summary: A new report suggests the unit, which employs 6,500 people, is on the verge of revolt.

data.title: Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to scam ‘hundreds of thousands of victims’ sued by Google
data.summary: The tech giant said a group called "Outsider Enterprise" used AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims, sending 2.5 million text messages over a span of two weeks.

data.title: Mistral is rumored to be raising €3B at €20B valuation
data.summary: The funding round would value the company at around €20 billion (about $23.15 billion), nearly double its Series C valuation of €11.7 billion.

FAANG had a good run, but a new acronym is taking over: MANGOS — Meta (or Microsoft, depending on who you ask), Anthropic, Nvidia, Google, OpenAI, and SpaceX.
